test_plan.strategy: Unit tests pin the new pure modules (semver comparator, artifact resolver, local-merge, layoutBase confinement, projection passthrough) with exhaustive vectors BEFORE the five version call-sites migrate (TRD risk flag 1). Integration tests exercise collectOne end-to-end per path: snapshot-first (stub aitri binary), fallback (no CLI), contained layout, remote sync. A committed golden fixture (pre-rc.41 legacy project + its recorded pre-change output) enforces NFR-041 byte-parity, with a mutation case proving the comparison is not vacuous. Regression NFR-042/043 are covered by the EXISTING admin-api e2e and remote-sync suites run with unmodified assertions — their TCs map onto those suites rather than duplicating them. NFR-046 (CI/CD) is enforced by the repository workflow executing the full suite on push; it is not TC-covered because its observable lives in the CI system, not the codebase. All fixtures use os.tmpdir()/AITRI_HUB_DIR isolation; no absolute user paths.
test_plan.coverage_goal: every MUST FR and every Regression NFR traced to at least one passing automated test; new modules at 100% branch coverage of their decision points

test_cases.title: semver: rc counters compare numerically
test_cases.given: lib/utils/semver.js is importable; inputs '2.0.0-rc.159' and '2.0.0-rc.15'
test_cases.then: return value > 0; also compareSemver('2.0.0-rc.15','2.0.0-rc.159') < 0 (antisymmetry)
test_cases.expected_result: rc.159 > rc.15 by numeric identifier comparison, not string order

test_cases.title: semver: pre-release precedence vs stable and across tags
test_cases.then: first returns < 0 (pre-release precedes stable); second returns < 0 ('alpha' < 'rc' alphanumerically)
test_cases.expected_result: semver.org §11 precedence holds for the identifier shapes Aitri emits

test_cases.title: semver: junk input is rejected, caller degrades
test_cases.given: input string 'git-deadbeef'
test_cases.when: parseSemver('git-deadbeef'); then aitri-version-reader processes CLI output 'git-deadbeef'
test_cases.then: parseSemver returns null; compareSemver('git-deadbeef','1.0.0') throws TypeError; version reader returns the same null/absent result it returns today for a missing CLI — no crash, no comparison performed
steps: parseSemver('git-deadbeef'); then aitri-version-reader processes CLI output 'git-deadbeef'
test_cases.expected_result: unparseable version → null path, never a fabricated compare

test_cases.title: fallback reads rc.41 names without aitri CLI
test_cases.given: temp project dir with .aitri (approvedPhases [1,2,3,4,5], artifactsDir 'spec'), spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json and spec/05_TRACEABILITY.json valid per contract; aitri CLI made unavailable (PATH without aitri)
test_cases.when: collectOne runs on the project (fallback path)
test_cases.then: record.compliance populated from 05_TRACEABILITY.json (overall_status + levels present); detectDrift hashes spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json for phase 4 and spec/05_TRACEABILITY.json for phase 5
steps: collectOne runs on the project (fallback path)
test_cases.expected_result: post-rc.41 project fully readable on the fallback path

test_cases.title: fallback: both names present → new name wins
test_cases.given: temp dir with spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json containing {"files":["new.js"]} AND spec/04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json containing {"files":["old.js"]}
test_cases.when: resolveArtifact(dir,'04_BUILD_REPORT.json') and the drift hash for phase 4
test_cases.then: resolved path ends with 04_BUILD_REPORT.json; hashed content is {"files":["new.js"]}; the old file is never opened
steps: resolveArtifact(dir,'04_BUILD_REPORT.json') and the drift hash for phase 4
test_cases.expected_result: new name wins when both exist

test_cases.title: fallback: neither name present degrades exactly as today
test_cases.given: temp dir with .aitri but no 04_*/05_* artifact under spec/
test_cases.when: compliance reader and drift detection run
test_cases.then: compliance section absent from the record; no exception thrown; no error entry in the collection log for the missing files
steps: compliance reader and drift detection run
test_cases.expected_result: absence degrades silently, matching current behavior

test_cases.title: local merge: lastSession read from .aitri.local
test_cases.given: temp project with .aitri lacking lastSession and .aitri.local containing {"lastSession":{"event":"verify-run","agent":"cli"}}
test_cases.then: state.lastSession.event === 'verify-run' (merged from .aitri.local)
test_cases.expected_result: per-machine fields merge over the shared view

test_cases.title: local merge: remote clone without .aitri.local
test_cases.given: temp project with .aitri only (no .aitri.local on disk), shaped like a fresh git clone
test_cases.then: no exception; returned state has NO lastSession key; no warning logged
test_cases.expected_result: absence is normal, nothing fabricated

test_cases.title: local merge: malformed .aitri.local ignored with warning
test_cases.given: temp project with valid .aitri (projectName 'p1') and .aitri.local containing '{invalid'
test_cases.then: returned state matches the shared .aitri view (projectName 'p1'); exactly one console.warn mentioning .aitri.local; no throw
test_cases.expected_result: corrupt per-machine file never breaks collection

test_cases.title: contained-layout project discovered and read end-to-end
test_cases.given: scan dir containing project 'cont1' whose .aitri declares layoutRoot 'aitri' and artifactsDir 'aitri/product/spec' (project-root-relative, per SCHEMA.md); artifacts exist at cont1/aitri/product/spec/; features at cont1/aitri/features/
test_cases.when: scan + collectOne run over the scan dir (fallback path)
test_cases.then: 'cont1' appears in the collected set; compliance read at projectDir + artifactsDir (never layoutRoot re-prefixed); features discovered under layoutRoot/features; pipeline phases read from .aitri
steps: scan + collectOne run over the scan dir (fallback path)
test_cases.expected_result: contained layout fully supported in discovery and reading

test_cases.title: flat project resolves identically (layoutBase = dir)
test_cases.given: temp flat project (no layoutRoot in .aitri) with spec/05_TRACEABILITY.json
test_cases.when: layoutBase(dir, state) and the compliance read
test_cases.then: layoutBase returns dir unchanged; artifact resolution is projectDir + artifactsDir — byte-identical to the pre-change code path
steps: layoutBase(dir, state) and the compliance read
test_cases.expected_result: flat layout regression-safe

test_cases.title: hostile layoutRoot rejected before any read
test_cases.given: temp project whose .aitri declares layoutRoot '../../etc'
test_cases.then: returns dir (layoutRoot ignored); one console.warn naming layoutRoot; instrumented fs access shows zero reads outside the project root
test_cases.expected_result: path escape rejected, collection continues as flat

test_cases.title: snapshot bugs.parseErrors → warning alert + unknown pill state
test_cases.given: a snapshot JSON fixture with bugs: {total:0, open:0, parseErrors:['root']}
test_cases.when: projectFromSnapshot + alert engine run on the fixture
test_cases.then: record.bugs.parseErrors deep-equals ['root']; alerts contain one severity 'warning' rule id BUGS_PARSE_ERROR whose message includes 'root' and 'NOT counted'
steps: projectFromSnapshot + alert engine run on the fixture
test_cases.expected_result: corrupt bug file is visible, not silent

test_cases.title: empty parseErrors adds no noise
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture with bugs: {total:2, open:1, parseErrors:[]} and a second fixture with a valid empty BUGS.json ({"bugs":[]}) on the fallback path
test_cases.then: record.bugs has NO parseErrors key in either case; no BUGS_PARSE_ERROR alert; fallback reports open=0 for the empty file
test_cases.expected_result: clean data stays clean — additive field appears only on real errors

test_cases.title: fallback flags non-contract BUGS.json shape
test_cases.given: temp project with BUGS.json containing {"bugs":42}
test_cases.when: bugs-reader runs on the fallback path
test_cases.then: reader returns parseErrors ['root'] (or the scope label); bug counters absent/zeroed AND the record carries the parseErrors marker; BUGS_PARSE_ERROR alert fires
steps: bugs-reader runs on the fallback path
test_cases.expected_result: shape violation = parse error, never '0 bugs'

test_cases.title: unbound results → warning indicator
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture whose resultsBinding reports state 'unbound' per rc.159 STATUS_JSON contract
test_cases.then: record.resultsBinding carried verbatim; RESULTS_UNBOUND alert (severity warning) present with message 'test results not bound to a verify run' and command 'aitri verify-run'
test_cases.expected_result: unbound evidence is flagged for the QA auditor

test_cases.title: older CLI without resultsBinding → nothing added
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture with NO resultsBinding key (pre-rc.148 CLI)
test_cases.then: record has NO resultsBinding key; no RESULTS_UNBOUND alert; no log entry
test_cases.expected_result: absent ≠ unbound

test_cases.title: malformed resultsBinding shape degrades to absent
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture with resultsBinding: 'yes' (wrong type: string instead of object)
test_cases.then: field is dropped (record has no resultsBinding); no crash; no alert
test_cases.expected_result: shape-checked passthrough never propagates garbage

test_cases.title: review stores full rc tag and silences the banner
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR temp home; detected CLI version '2.0.0-rc.159'
test_cases.when: run 'aitri-hub integration review 2.0.0-rc.159', then generate the dashboard
test_cases.then: integration-compat.json has reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.159' (full tag, untruncated); dashboard.json contains no integrationAlert
steps: run 'aitri-hub integration review 2.0.0-rc.159', then generate the dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: reviewed-up-to expresses pre-release versions

test_cases.title: newer unreviewed pre-release re-raises the banner
test_cases.given: manifest reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.159'; detected CLI '2.0.0-rc.160'
test_cases.then: guard reports the CLI as newer than the baseline (compareSemver > 0) and integrationAlert is produced
test_cases.expected_result: rc.160 > rc.159 detected numerically

test_cases.title: review rejects an unparseable version
test_cases.given: AITRI_HUB_DIR temp home with an existing manifest reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.159'
test_cases.then: exit code !== 0; stderr/stdout contains a usage message; integration-compat.json bytes unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: invalid input cannot corrupt the baseline

test_cases.title: quality gates + coverage surfaces carried through
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture containing quality-gate results [{name:'lint',passed:true},{name:'security',passed:false}] and ac_coverage + coverage-audit freshness fields per rc.159 STATUS_JSON
test_cases.then: record carries the gates array with names and pass/fail intact, plus the ac_coverage and coverage-audit fields verbatim
test_cases.expected_result: downstream views can render without another collector change

test_cases.title: absent surfaces add no fields
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture without quality-gate/ac_coverage/coverage-audit fields
test_cases.then: record contains none of the new keys (deep-equal to pre-change projection for this fixture)
test_cases.expected_result: additive and absent-tolerant

test_cases.title: garbage shapes are dropped, not carried
test_cases.given: snapshot fixture with quality gates as a 3 MB string and ac_coverage as a function-like object
test_cases.then: fields dropped (shape check fails); no crash; record otherwise complete
test_cases.expected_result: shape-checked passthrough

test_cases.title: eligible project collected via snapshot only
test_cases.given: fixture project with .aitri aitriVersion '2.0.0-rc.159' and a stub 'aitri' binary on PATH returning a valid snapshot; fallback readers instrumented
test_cases.then: record built from the stub snapshot (source marker 'snapshot'); instrumented fallback artifact readers record ZERO invocations
test_cases.expected_result: snapshot-first is never demoted (FR-017)

test_cases.title: below-floor version routes to fallback
test_cases.given: fixture project with .aitri aitriVersion '0.1.50' (< 0.1.77 floor)
test_cases.then: gteSemver('0.1.50','0.1.77') is false → legacy fallback path selected, same as today
test_cases.expected_result: floor semantics unchanged under the new comparator

test_cases.title: snapshot failure degrades visibly; fallback fills in, snapshot data never overridden
test_cases.given: eligible fixture whose stub 'aitri' exits 1 on status --json; .aitri and artifacts present on disk
test_cases.then: record carries the degradation marker (degradationReason non-null); record fields ARE populated from the fallback readers (FR-017 degradation path); the card renders the degraded state
test_cases.expected_result: failure is visible degradation with fallback data — never a silent empty record; a SUCCESSFUL snapshot is never overridden by fallback data (NFR-040)

test_cases.title: legacy golden fixture: record equality
test_cases.given: in-repo golden fixture: pre-rc.41 flat project (04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json, 05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json, lastSession inside .aitri) + its recorded pre-change collected record (timestamps excluded)
test_cases.when: collectOne runs on the fixture with the new code
test_cases.then: collected record deep-equals the golden record field-for-field (timestamps excluded)
steps: collectOne runs on the fixture with the new code
test_cases.expected_result: legacy path byte-parity holds

test_cases.title: legacy fixture with feature subdirs stays equal
test_cases.given: same golden fixture plus features/f1/.aitri and features/f1/spec/05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json
test_cases.then: feature summary section of the record deep-equals the golden feature section
test_cases.expected_result: feature scanning unchanged for legacy projects

test_cases.title: golden comparison actually detects a change
test_cases.given: golden fixture with one field mutated in the freshly collected record (e.g. compliance.overall_status flipped)
test_cases.then: the assertion FAILS and names the differing path — proving the parity test is not vacuous
test_cases.expected_result: the golden test can catch a real regression

test_cases.title: admin CRUD over loopback unchanged
test_cases.given: aitri-hub web running on 127.0.0.1:3099 with AITRI_HUB_DIR temp home (existing e2e harness)
test_cases.then: POST returns 200/201 and GET lists p1 — the pre-existing admin-api e2e suite passes with UNMODIFIED assertions
test_cases.expected_result: admin surface untouched

test_cases.title: PUT and DELETE lifecycle unchanged
test_cases.given: running server with project p1 registered
test_cases.then: PUT returns 200 with name 'p2'; DELETE returns 204 No Content (the API's existing contract); GET no longer lists it (existing suite assertions unmodified)
test_cases.expected_result: full CRUD lifecycle intact

test_cases.title: non-loopback request still 403
test_cases.given: running server; request crafted with Host header / origin simulating non-loopback client per the existing suite
test_cases.then: response status 403 (existing assertion, unmodified)
test_cases.expected_result: localhost-only guard intact
test_cases.manual_reason: The localhost-only guard keys on req.socket.remoteAddress (lib/commands/web.js:118-124); a loopback-only e2e harness cannot present a non-loopback source address without privileged network spoofing. Verified by code inspection: server binds 127.0.0.1 and the guard 403s any remoteAddress outside {127.0.0.1, ::1, ::ffff:127.0.0.1}.

test_cases.title: remote clone/pull cycle unchanged
test_cases.given: existing remote-sync test fixture (local bare git repo registered as type 'remote')
test_cases.then: cycle 1 clones into ~/.aitri-hub/cache/<slug>-<id>; cycle 2 pulls; existing suite assertions pass unmodified
test_cases.expected_result: remote sync intact

test_cases.title: poller skip on unchanged content
test_cases.given: github-poller fixture reporting unchanged raw content
test_cases.then: no git pull is executed this cycle (existing assertion unmodified)
test_cases.expected_result: cheap-poll optimization intact

test_cases.title: 429 backoff preserved
test_cases.given: poller fixture returning HTTP 429
test_cases.then: backoff state recorded and no pull attempted until the backoff window passes (existing assertion unmodified)
test_cases.expected_result: rate-limit behavior intact

test_cases.title: current-release fields all present with same types
test_cases.given: an unchanged fixture project collected pre-change, its field/type inventory recorded
test_cases.when: the same fixture is collected with the new code and the schema-diff helper compares
test_cases.then: every recorded field exists with the same JS type; diff report empty
steps: the same fixture is collected with the new code and the schema-diff helper compares
test_cases.expected_result: no field removed or retyped

test_cases.title: new fields appear only with data present
test_cases.given: fixture snapshot WITHOUT parseErrors/resultsBinding/quality gates
test_cases.then: record contains none of the new keys
test_cases.expected_result: old consumers see an unchanged record

test_cases.title: schema-diff helper detects a type change
test_cases.given: a record where bugs.open is artificially set to the string '3'
test_cases.when: schema-diff helper compares against the inventory
test_cases.then: diff reports bugs.open type change number→string — proving the guard is not vacuous
steps: schema-diff helper compares against the inventory
test_cases.expected_result: the additive gate can catch a real break

test_cases.title: safe relative layoutRoot accepted
test_cases.given: temp project, .aitri layoutRoot 'aitri' (plain child dir, exists)
test_cases.then: returns dir/aitri; zero warnings
test_cases.expected_result: legitimate contained layout passes confinement

test_cases.title: dangling layoutRoot does not throw
test_cases.given: temp project, .aitri layoutRoot 'aitri' but the directory does not exist yet
test_cases.then: no exception (deepest-existing-ancestor realpath check); returns the resolved child path or dir per design; collection continues
test_cases.expected_result: missing dir ≠ crash

test_cases.title: absolute, ../ and symlink escapes all rejected
test_cases.given: three temp projects: layoutRoot '/etc'; layoutRoot '../../etc'; layoutRoot 'link' where link is a symlink to a dir outside the project root
test_cases.then: each returns dir (field ignored) with one warning; instrumented fs shows zero reads outside each project root
test_cases.expected_result: all three escape vectors blocked (NFR-045 trio)
